stories.title: World Cup Final Watch Party at Weidner Field
stories.angle: The Colorado Springs Switchbacks are hosting a FREE public watch party at Weidner Field today (Sunday, July 19) for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final — Spain vs. Argentina. Doors open for 12:30 PM kickoff. Fans can watch on the Jumbotron from the Phil Long Club or on the pitch itself. Concessions available, cornhole and lawn games provided. Free and open to all.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
TODAY: Free World Cup Final watch party at Weidner Field — Spain vs. Argentina on the Jumbotron. ⚽

Kickoff 12:30 PM MT. On the pitch or in the Phil Long Club. Cornhole, lawn games, concessions.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The biggest soccer match in the world is playing in Colorado Springs today.

Switchbacks are hosting a free World Cup Final watch party at Weidner Field — 12:30 PM MT, open to all. 🌎⚽

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your family has been following the 2026 World Cup, today's the day — and there's a free place to watch it right here in Colorado Springs.

The Colorado Springs Switchbacks are hosting a free, public watch party at Weidner Field for the FIFA World Cup Final: Spain vs. Argentina. Kickoff is at 12:30 PM MT. You can watch on the Jumbotron from the Phil Long Club or right out on the pitch itself. The Switchbacks are providing cornhole and lawn games, and concessions will be available for purchase.

It's free and open to everyone. No tickets, no reservations — just show up. This is one of those rare moments where you can watch the biggest soccer match in the world with thousands of your neighbors on a real soccer pitch.

Are your kiddos heading to Weidner Field today? ⚽
facebook.image_caption: Free World Cup Final watch party at Weidner Field — Spain vs. Argentina, 12:30 PM MT. Free entry, lawn games, and the Jumbotron. Colorado Springs showing up for the biggest match of the year. ⚽🌎

article.headline: Free World Cup Final Watch Party at Weidner Field This Sunday

stories.title: Button Battery Toy Recall Alert
stories.angle: CPSC has issued multiple recalls in 2026 for children's toys containing button/coin cell batteries that children can easily access. Products include LED finger lights (Oitnlaughter brand, sold on Amazon July 2025–April 2026 for ~$14) and LED light-up tutu skirts (sold by Bmrwtg on Amazon). Swallowing a button cell battery can cause internal chemical burns and death. Stop use immediately; contact sellers for full refunds.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
RECALL: CPSC recalled LED finger light toys and light-up tutu skirts — button batteries inside are easily accessed and can be swallowed. ⚠️

Swallowing a button battery causes internal burns and death. Stop use now.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Amazon LED finger light toys recalled — button batteries kids can swallow. Death and internal burns possible. ⚠️

Oitnlaughter brand, 36-pack, ~$14, sold July 2025–April 2026. Contact Delightmi for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
There's a pattern of recalls you need to know about right now. The CPSC has recalled multiple children's toys in 2026 for the same reason: button cell batteries that kids can easily remove, put in their mouths, and swallow. The consequences can be fatal.

The two most recent ones to flag: Oitnlaughter LED Finger Lights (sets of 36 in animal designs, sold on Amazon by Delightmi from July 2025 through April 2026 for about $14) and Children's LED Tutu Skirts (sold on Amazon by Bmrwtg). Both were recalled because the button batteries powering the lights are accessible to children. If a button or coin cell battery is swallowed, it can cause internal chemical burns and death.

If you have either of these products, stop using them immediately and take them away from your kids. For the finger lights, contact Delightmi for a full refund — you'll be asked to dispose of the toys and send a photo confirmation to the company.

The broader pattern here is worth flagging: the CPSC has issued at least five similar recalls in 2026, all involving inexpensive LED toys from Amazon that contain accessible button batteries. When you're buying toys online, this is something to look for. ⚠️

Which of these products do you recognize? Let us know in the comments so other families can check too.
facebook.image_caption: CPSC has recalled LED finger light toys and light-up tutu skirts — button batteries inside can be accessed by children. Swallowing one can cause serious injury or death. Stop use and request a refund. ⚠️

article.headline: Button Battery Toy Recalls: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:

stories.title: Back to School 2026 — COS District Date Guide
stories.angle: With summer winding down, COS families have roughly 2–4 weeks until the first school bell. D49 starts August 3 (kindergarteners, 6th graders, and 9th graders) and August 4 (all students); D11 starts August 12; D20 (Academy) starts August 13. D11 is partnering with Edukit to provide free, grade-specific supply kits to more than 14,000 K–8 students — delivered on the first day of school, no cost to families. D11 enrollment: online at d11.org or in person at 1033 N. Franklin St., M–F 8 AM–4 PM MT.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
COS back-to-school dates 2026 🎒

D49: August 4 (Aug. 3 for K, 6th, and 9th graders)
D11: August 12
D20: August 13

2–4 weeks away. Check registration, immunization records, and supply lists now.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Summer isn't over, but back to school is closer than it feels. Here are the 2026 first-day dates for the three main Colorado Springs districts:

📅 D49 (Falcon School District 49): August 4
📅 D11 (Colorado Springs School District 11): August 12
📅 D20 (Academy School District 20): August 13

If you're in D49, you're about two weeks out. D11 and D20 families have a little more time, but not as much as it seems.

A few things worth checking this week: Is your child enrolled? D11 has online enrollment at d11.org, or you can go in person to 1033 N. Franklin St. (weekdays, 8 AM–4 PM MT). Immunization records — Colorado requires them updated before the school year. School supply lists for most COS schools are posted on district and school websites. And if your kids are taking district transportation, registration is usually done separately and fills up.

D11 also has Monday late starts built into its calendar, so worth knowing before carpools and pickup schedules get locked in.

What's on your back-to-school list? 🎒
facebook.image_caption: COS back-to-school 2026: D49 starts Aug. 4, D11 starts Aug. 12, D20 starts Aug. 13. Two to four weeks away — time to check on enrollment, immunization records, and supply lists.

article.headline: COS Back-to-School 2026: First-Day Dates for D11, D20, and D49

stories.title: Wade Logan Annyka Fabric Dresser Recall
stories.angle: The CPSC has recalled the Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dresser (sold on Wayfair, September 2023–May 2026, ~$98) due to tip-over and entrapment hazards — a risk of serious injury or death to children if the dresser is not anchored to the wall. Violates the STURDY Act mandatory standard. Consumers should stop using the dresser if unanchored, move it out of children's reach, and contact the importer for refund instructions (email annykarecall@yeah.net with disposal photo).

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dresser (Wayfair, Sept 2023–May 2026, ~$98). ⚠️

Unanchored, it can tip over and trap a child. Stop use if not wall-anchored. Contact the importer for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you have a Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dresser in your home — especially in a child's room — please check it right now.

The CPSC has recalled this dresser because it can tip over if it's not anchored to the wall, creating an entrapment hazard that can seriously injure or kill a child. The dresser was sold on Wayfair from September 2023 through May 2026 for about $98. It comes in black, brown, gray, and white and has nine fabric drawers. If yours isn't secured to the wall, stop using it and move it somewhere kids can't reach.

This recall was issued under the STURDY Act, which sets mandatory safety standards for clothing storage units. If your dresser isn't anchored, contact the importer (Ningbo Jiangdong Peter International Trading) for refund instructions.

Tip-over furniture remains one of the most consistent child safety hazards in American homes. If you have any free-standing furniture — dressers, bookshelves, wardrobes — in rooms where kids spend time, this is a good reminder to check anchoring on everything, recalled or not.

Have you already anchored the furniture in your kids' rooms? ⚠️
facebook.image_caption: RECALL: Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dresser (sold on Wayfair, Sept. 2023–May 2026, ~$98). Unanchored, it can tip over and trap a child. Stop use if unanchored. Contact importer for refund. ⚠️

article.headline: Wade Logan Annyka Fabric Dresser Recalled for Tip-Over Hazard

stories.title: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Turns 100 This Year
stories.angle: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo celebrates its 100th anniversary in 2026 and was just named the #2 Best Zoo in the US by USA TODAY's 10Best Readers' Choice Awards. Open daily 9 AM–5 PM (last admission 4 PM). Summer admission: adults $34.75, children ages 3–11 $29.75 (peak Fri/Sat/Sun late May–September). Advance timed tickets required. Hand-feed giraffes, watch grizzlies swim, walk with African penguins. A great COS summer bucket list stop.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Cheyenne Mountain Zoo turns 100 in 2026 — and just ranked #2 Best Zoo in the US (USA TODAY). 🦒

Open daily 9 AM–5 PM. Adults $34.75 / kids ages 3–11 $29.75 (peak weekends). Advance tickets required.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
We have a 100-year-old zoo in our backyard, and it just got a major national shoutout.

Cheyenne Mountain Zoo turns 100 in 2026, and USA TODAY's 10Best Readers' Choice Awards just ranked it the #2 Best Zoo in the entire country. If you've been putting a zoo trip on the summer bucket list, this is a good reason to actually go.

What makes it worth the visit with kids: you can hand-feed one of the largest reticulated giraffe herds in the world, watch grizzlies swim (they fish for trout), walk alongside African penguins, and get hands-on in The Loft. There's also an antique carousel, a sky ride to the summit, and the Grizzly Grill for lunch. Summer programming includes camps, Starlight Safaris, and animal encounters — worth checking the calendar at cmzoo.org.

A few logistics: hours are 9 AM–5 PM daily, last admission at 4 PM. Peak pricing (Fridays, Saturdays, Sundays, late May through September) is $34.75 for adults and $29.75 for kids ages 3 to 11. Advance, timed tickets are required for both members and non-members — you can't just show up and walk in. 🦒

Have you taken your kids to the zoo this summer? What's their favorite stop?
facebook.image_caption: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo turns 100 in 2026 and ranked #2 Best Zoo in the US. Open daily 9 AM–5 PM, $34.75 adults / $29.75 kids ages 3–11 (peak). Advance tickets required at cmzoo.org. 🦒

article.headline: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Turns 100 in 2026 — and Just Ranked No. 2 in the US
